messages.content: Mission target: 192.168.56.101. Begin the engagement now. Spawn the appropriate first agent.

{"thought": "SITUATION: Starting engagement against 192.168.56.101. No scan results available yet. I am in OSINT phase but need to identify the target's attack surface before any exploitation. MISSION OBJECTIVE: Find flag.txt and show its contents. ALLOWED: exploitation, lateral. HYPOTHESIS: Cannot exploit without knowing what's running. Must enumerate open ports and services first. DECISION: Spawn a scanner agent to perform TCP/UDP port scan and service version detection. This follows the PTES methodology: reconnaissance comes before exploitation. A scanner will reveal the attack surface and allow me to prioritize high-confidence vectors.", "action": "spawn_agent", "parameters": {"agent_type": "scanner", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "scan_tcp_udp_full", "options": {"tcp_ports": "1-10000", "udp_ports": "161,69,123,5353", "detect_versions": true, "detect_os": true}}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Scanner agent spawned with ID scanner-c277b3bd. This is a full TCP/UDP scan that will take some time. HYPOTHESIS: Need to wait for scan results before making exploitation decisions. This is mandatory per PTES methodology — know everything before exploiting. DECISION: Call wait_for_agents to block until scan completes. The scan will reveal open ports, service versions, and OS fingerprint — all critical for choosing the right exploitation path toward finding flag.txt.", "action": "wait_for_agents", "parameters": {"agent_ids": ["scanner-c277b3bd"], "timeout": 1200}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Scan complete. 192.168.56.101 is Metasploitable2 with 27 open ports. Critical findings: vsftpd 2.3.4 (backdoor CVE-2011-2523), Samba 3.X (usermap_script CVE-2007-2447), distccd (CVE-2004-2687), UnrealIRCd (backdoor CVE-2010-2075), r-services (512-514), MySQL/PostgreSQL (default creds likely), VNC (5900), bindshell on 1524. HYPOTHESIS: Multiple paths to shell exist. My playbook confirms vsftpd, Samba, distccd, and UnrealIRCd are proven vectors on Metasploitable2. OPTIONS: A) Spawn ALL exploit agents in parallel — maximum coverage, first shell wins. B) Try only vsftpd — single point of failure, wastes parallel capacity. DECISION: Option A. Per methodology: exploit multiple vectors simultaneously. Spawn agents for vsftpd, samba, distccd, unrealircd, and webapp scan on ports 80/8180 in parallel. Web scanning is slow so it starts early.", "action": "spawn_agents_batch", "parameters": {"agents": [{"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_vsftpd", "options": {"port": 21}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_samba", "options": {"port": 445}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_distcc", "options": {"port": 3632}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_unrealircd", "options": {"port": 6667}}, {"agent_type": "webapp", "target": "http://192.168.56.101:80", "task_type": "web_scan_full"}, {"agent_type": "webapp", "target": "http://192.168.56.101:8180", "task_type": "web_scan_full"}]}}
